methods.description: This method compares the diatom flora of Ile Amsterdam with those of other sub-Antarctic islands using the Community Coefficient of Sørensen to assess species similarity.

steps.description: The Community Coefficient of Sørensen (1948) was used with the formula: 2c/(a+b+2c), where a and b are the number of species observed exclusively in each of the two sites and c is the number of species shared between these sites.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Sørensen similarity coefficient for diatom species

steps.description: Datasets were made taxonomically consistent prior to the similarity analysis.

methods.description: This method evaluates the sampling effort's representation of the moss-inhabiting diatom flora of Ile Amsterdam using species richness estimators.

steps.description: The incidence-based species richness estimator (ICE) and the mean Chao2 richness estimator were calculated using the EstimateS program v.9.1.0.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate species richness using ICE and Chao2 estimators

methods.description: NMDS is used to reduce the multidimensional species data matrix into two dimensions to reflect site dissimilarities based on diatom species composition.

steps.description: NMDS was performed based on Bray-Curtis (Sørensen) dissimilarity calculated on square root transformed species data.

methods.method_name: Agglomerative Clustering and Indicator Species Analysis
methods.description: This method groups diatom assemblages using clustering and identifies indicator species for site groups.

steps.description: Classical agglomerative clustering was used to group diatom assemblages according to Bray-Curtis distances using the unweighted per group average algorithm (UPGMA).
Indicator species were identified using the extended Indicator Species Analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: identify indicator species using Indicator Species Analysis

methods.description: This method describes the collection and analysis of moss samples for diatom study.

steps.description: In total, 148 moss samples were collected during a field campaign in November and December 2007.
Samples were collected in PVC bottles and fixed with 3% formaldehyde.
When possible, the samples were geographically localised using GPS, accompanied by a detailed site description.
steps.searchable_summary: record GPS location and site description

steps.description: pH, conductivity, and water temperature were measured in situ using a WTW 340i Multimeter.
Cl-, Na+, K+, Mg2+, and Ca2+ were analysed at the Laboratory for Ecosystem Management using a continuous flow analysis (CFA-SKALAR).
